text: This is by far the worst experience I’ve ever had with cellular service. Tmobile has the worst coverage I’ve ever seen. Almost everywhere I go it’ll say “5G” and even have all four bars but each call whether it’s a video or just audio sounds completely choppy and ends up being pointless to even try to decipher what the other person is saying. Sometimes it works, sometimes it doesn’t. Completely depends on the day, because I can be in the exact same area where it’s dead one day and then works perfectly fine another day. Absolutely horrible.

I went to the store on Roosevelt in St Petersburg, Fl to change to T-Mobile 2 line account.  After two hours in the store, I found out the sales person gave me false information about the discount on a phone that had to be ordered.  The phone that I did leave the store with was suspended.  So I had to return to the store to get phone unsuspended.  Later, I spent time on the phone with T-Mobile to get a second phone.  That employee also quoted a discounted price for a phone that was not actually discounted.  WARNING: Carefully review the finance agreement they will email to you for electronic signature.  Likely, it will be the full price and not the false discount price the employee told you.  T-Mobile employees talk a good game but they cannot back it up with actual good service.
When it comes to having different payment methods and options T-mobile DOES NOT value it’s customers, so please for ANYONE thinking about using T-Mobile, DON’T, HUGE WASTE of MONEY, TIME AND EFFORT

I sent this to supposedly an email address for complaints and no response.
My Wife, Mother in Law, and I all recently switched to your company under one family plan. We dealt with the store located at 7622 Mall Rd Florence, Ky. We were told by a night time assistant manager multiple times we would receive $500 credit per phone if we traded in our old smart phones. We signed up for three S21s. They were not in stock and he personally promised to have the phones in store within two weeks. I showed up at the two week mark and he denied to the manager that we spoke with, that he made that promise. The second manager got our phones from a different store. The first assistant manager that got us signed up told us more than once we could pay the phones off early without penalty; this was his second lie to us! Also, another employee told us we could pay the phones off early without penalty. When we inquired later about paying the phones off early another employee said we wouldn't get our $500 per phone credit if we paid the phones off earlier than two years. We have been deceived multiple times by this store from two different employees and we have no service at our home in Independence ky and very little anywhere else. We are requesting to pay the phones off early and wanting our $1500 credit.

I purchased a phone and new coverage, Issues with dropped calls.  Went to a t mobile office and rep said he updated and fixed issued..this was b4 14 day return.  A week later went back with same problem wanting to exchange phone at Baldwin park facility,. Now I'm told too late due to passed 14 days ..don't forget I had already addressed the issue within proper time frame.  They would not resolve issue.  I had Verizon for 30 years.  T mobile is cheaper but you get what you pay for...

T mobile is the worst .,I transferred over from Verizon. We I went to Verizon, it was because of the senior plan they offered. I continue with the enrollment, because I knew the service was the best. So, I went to T-Mobile , because they offered a senior plan. I also transferred my service, because I could keep my telephone number and they would , purchase my phone. Sound like a good deal. Well, I found out later, there was more to it. After waiting to send the phone, I heard nothing. I did not know anything. I found out only because Verizon contacted me. They were trying to get the phone. When I called T-Mobile. I was told it had passed the allotted time of 60 days. And there was. Otho good I could do but pay for the Verizon phone.,I was never informed about the timing . So, now I have two phones. Why do I need to phones. The are the worst company and scammers. They need to be stopped. I have talked to many people. I thought , their action was because of me being a senior. They take advantage, because they have an monopoly on the senior plan. But, as I  talked to others. They have unfair practices with anyone.

I have 6 lines with Sprint/T-Mobile.  Started with Sprint,  when they merged with T-Mobile my service became pure junk. I've been in to the store several times, get nothing but excuses. I will be canceling all my phone services as soon as my contract expires. Maybe after enough customers cancel they will fix their crappie service.

I had t mobile 6 yes ago and the service... well there was no service... it was the WORST cell phone service I've ever had.  Then I switched to T mobile and was very happy.  Now T mobile owns sprint so they sent me a new sim card for "faster and better service" and I am back to having the WORST I've ever had.  I can't call,  text,  use the internet,  wifi, nothing. This is BS. What am I paying for....I was just recently in another state and STILL had no service whatsoever.  I'm extremely frustrated and I do not recommend t mobile to anyone.  I am switching ASAP

T-Mobile does NOT value their customers, buyers beware!!! As you know T-Mobile was hacked and how did we learn, a text message that my information is on the dark web. Because of that I had to purchase service from a credit monitoring company. So silly me, I thought that since am paying to monitor my credit after their breech, maybe T-Mobile could help me with getting another phone, mind you I didn’t ask for a new phone but maybe an older model iPhone. They said no because no promotions. I have had the same phone, a J7, for almost 5 years. I don’t call in and I have never asked for Anything! I would like for T-Mobile to pay for me the cost of the monitoring service or an IPhone at no cost. What do I need to do to persue this matter further? A lawyer??

Tmobile customer service is a joke. Have been calling for over a month. Every time I call I get a different story and every time they say they will call me back, they never do. As soon as my contract is over I am switching back to AT&T. My bill might be a bit higher but the cell service and customer service is SO much better #tmobilesucks

I don't write reviews at all but, i think this time t- mobile deserved it. To 
Start off with I was a customer for 13 plus years and i had 5 family lines of service with them so my bill was of over 400 every month, so you do the math. So i decided to switch carrier in order to have better service and save some $$$ ,
 t-mobile got mad and decided to locked my phone that was already paid off ( got it as a christmas gift from my chidren) and also sent me to collection for 303 dlls for some antennas that they gave me 6 years ago. And now they wanted them back or have to pay this amount.... really?after 6 years? ....Whatever.

 Custumer service also said that they were gonna unlock my phone 3 times  after i was waiting for over 1 hour every time just to have someone on the phone , and nothing , phone still locked and of course paid for. So they LIED everytime.

If you really want to:

overpay every month, 

End up in collection 

Lose your equipment for silly reasons

Have a liar customer service

T- mobile is the company.

I was with Sprint and never had a complaint but since Tmoble took over there coverage sucks. I didn’t have service in Sevierville or Dandridge, TN today. Very limited service in Lenoir City, TN also. Think it’s time to find a better company to switch too.

I'm not one to give negative reviews but this has to be said. As Sprint customers we are forced into switching to the worse service ever. 5g is a joke and when sending text messages they barely go through. How long will it take for t-mobile to get it right. We are switching to Verizon at the end of our contract.

I switched from T-Mobile to Verizon for better service for my job.  T-Mobile did not give me the courtesy of sending me one final bill which I needed for Verizon to pay that bill off it is now been two months and they have sent me to collection. When I contacted T-Mobile to ask Why they did not send me a final bill, the guy was nothing but rude. I asked a simple question as to why I did not receive a final bill before they sent me to collections and he didn’t have a good answer other than to tell me to contact collections not happy would not recommend. I need to add I was also a customer for 13 years so it’s not like I jump ship after a short bit of time and was usually very happy with them but this left a horrible taste in my mouth

I had to leave T-Mobile after they merged with Sprint because service was so terrible. Not to mention, I got charged for an entire billing month after switching plus a late fee. Customer service was zero help. Never using TMobile again.

I am a Sprint customer and, of course, I need to swith to T-mobile now that they merged. On top of that I received a letter from T-mobile saying I need to buy a new phone because my old one, Samsung Galaxy S7 won't be compatible with them starting January, 2022. I went to the store and wanted to upgrade to Samsung S21. The promotion that they have is a discount of $500 in exchange for the old phone, but because my old phone has a little crack on the screen (you can barely see it), they don't want it. Now they want me to pay the full amount $1,100 or buy a cheaper phone. I have been with Sprint for 12 years in a row. Never had any problems at all. Now I am been penalized because of a stupid crack on my screen, no promotions for me, period. This is the way that T-mobile is saying welcome to the Sprint new customers. Wow! By the way I called the company by phone and they said I needed to "set up an appointment with Tmobile to have a special promotion for Sprint customers: Sprint Select and to mentioned that so they don't ask for my old phone. I went to the store, again, the employee didn't have any idea of an appointment and he told me that the employee on the phone was wrong, that for the promotion I have to give them my phone which they don't want because of the crack. Don't go to Tmobile!!!!! Worst company.!!!!!!

I left Verizon for what was supposed to be better 5G with Tmobile and lower price. Sure my phone may show I always have 5G. But it's a lie I never have a good phone connection if at all. Not even in my living room.  I am on wifi right now actually. My bill went from 110 to 160 per month after the 15 days. They made an offer for my Verizon 5G phone. But only paid out half what they offered. They have my old Note phone. I will not be getting that back. The customer service phones are answered in the Philippines. I'm cool with that. But sometimes thier accents are hard to understand. But at least they are Americans. That is my 1 good point about T Mobile. Other than that they are horrible in every other way. Stay away is my best advice from that sucker who believed their lies.

T-Mobile is not even worth a one star....
From day one I had problems with service, calls dropped, no able to answer calls and the list goes on. I dealt with this for a little over a year. I called and would be told its the towers and one will be going up soon and the coverage will get better. Never did then I was told its my device. Changed and had to pay for a new phone and got the same crap service. Working out of town was disaster. Then when i finally left i was told it would cost me 220$ for the last month and the device payment or 110 for the last months and turn the device in. I kept the phone and then they said its now 330$. I paid for months 110$ for a phone that hardly worked when i needed it and now they tac on 100$ what in the actual hell.... I am not one to do reviews but i feel the next person needs to be warned. This Business is terrible. Maybe its just in Montana? idk but steer clear unless you want to be forced to pay for crap service.

This is intended for the TMobile located in Chester Virginia. I’m not sure if they will ever see this but Debbie is the rudest person I have ever met in my entire life. She has a horrible attitude, is rude to the customers and has no place working in customer service considering she lacks customer skills and good service. My overall rating of her would be an F. If you see her in the store I would advise you to leave immediately or call the police, she gets fiesty and bold.
If you can go with another carrier DO NOT choose TMobile they do NOT work with customers

I cracked my screen 6 months ago. I pay for insurance every month in case this ever happens. Apparently the type of screen I have is on back order and my only option was to get a loaner phone so I could ship mine into a warehouse to get it fixed. I still have yet to get a loaner phone because of crappy customer service. I've called 4 different stores and each store tells me something different. Each one has been no help. Sprint would of had this fixed the week it happened!!

They are the worst company I've ever run into not only do the representatives of little knowledge of what they're doing they make mistakes that are unfixable... They say they'll help they'll help they'll help and in the end the customer is the one that gets screwed regardless... And to make matters even worse they represent having the telephone that they don't even carry you have to wait several and I mean several weeks to even see such phone... Horrible service

EVERY LAST T-MOBILE EMPLOYEE IS A LYING EXTORTIONIST C&NT!

F&CKING DIE!

I was with T-mobile for more than 10 yrs. In Dec 2020 they capped my unlimited hotspot plan, such that it turns to 2G (not even 3G) when it hits 2 or 5GB. So I could no longer use it. I cancelled. Four months later they billed me, I paid it thinking that it was just a left over balance. But then I got another bill. I called them and the rep said that it is indeed their mistake, 2 other lines in my account wasn't cancelled (so what happened with the 3 months that they didn't bill me?). So I asked them to refund the $45 that I had mistakenly paid. When they didn't refund me, I asked my credit card company to reverse the charges (disputed). Then T-mobile sent it to collections.
I was told that I would save on my bill from switching sprint to T-Mobile! I’m actually paying more if this was the case I should of kept my same plan T-Mobile is a bunch of schemes

Absolutely the worst customer service and cell service I have ever had. From day one, dealt with poor service to where my boyfriend and I could be in the same room and the call would not go through but the service would show full bars, etc. 

Spent many many many hours on the phone with their customer service explaining our issues we were having only to be ignored or brushed off. Promised call backs that never happened, lied about expectations and what would happen, absolute ZERO communication throughout the company. 

Every time we called, the person before would not leave any notes so we would have to start from scratch every time we called, re-explaining everything. 

Cut-to months later, we had it with trying their "fixes" and just wanted to cancel our service. We were promised that for our suffering, they would cover our last bill and would have our servicetermination fees waved. Service was turned off and we asked multiple times if we had any bill that needed to be paid and always got a no for an answer. Then one day got a bill in the mail for over $1000!!!! They never covered our last bill and said we ended our service early so we needed to pay! 

I absolutely will NEVER recommend Tmobile for any reason whatsoever.

TMobile does offer Military discounts. Aside from that their service has absolutely tanked. Coverage while traveling is horrible in so many areas between Northern Utah, along I-80 through Wyoming, Nebraska, Iowa, also up through Northeast Wyoming and into and through South Dakota. Don't forget their extremely lousy security of our personal information and device information that gives Identity thieves and hackers all they need. Basically nothing from TMobile after the latest hack. 5G service is false and certainly no better than 4G if even that fast. My OnePlus 9 Pro when in TMobile coverage area shows a 5G connection but is still slow. Sorry but they paid Billions to buy Sprint and we as consumers got no improvement in service as a result.
Very unhappy with T mobile.  Switched from Sprint to get Magenta plan for 2 lines   Terrible service I my home, can't access Apple TV free for a year.  Wish I could go back to Sprint.

If I could leave no stars I would. Having ported out 2 numbers from Verizon back in May to an existing account with Sprint, O traded 3 phones in at the time for credits applied to the new lease payments.  Now 3 months later I find that only 2 of them received it and my Galaxy smartphone is being charged full price. It seems the store never advised me there was no promotion available for my Note 10 towards the new phone. So they stole my phone, I ended paying Verizon over 600 to pay off the remaining balance and no one seems to know where it ended up. Nothing  but excuses or we'll look into it. Meanwhile I am out a 1100 dollar phone, horrible reception, and a nearly 200 phone bill each month. I will be going back to Verizon,  even though it will cost me and would advise anyone reading this to avoid Tmobile.

I called Tmobile and told them I had my own device and wanted to switch over to them, they sent me a sim card for my device and I changed my mind because of the terrible customer service they have!  I was told that I woud get refunded the activation charge back but instead they billed me another 20.00 bucks bringing the total to 50.00, Its now almost a month and still no refund!  Stay away from T Mobile crappy company , they took the money and ran!!! Thank God it was only 50.00 could have been a lot more
T-Mobile is very very veeeeerry trash. Calls drop u can’t call out the internet slow. Also no connection and that’s even when ur in a T-Mobile store. I want out of my contract and away from T-Mobile

AfterPurchasingAGalaxyA12 At
TheTMobileSpringfieldStore
       (8116)  (610)543-2608
         Representative 815 
IWentToTheWoodlynPaStore
(429E)ToInquireAboutDownLoad
ingFontsToMyNewPhone.

TimeToAssistMe&HelpWithThis
Request.HeWasVeryHelpful,Kind
&PatientWithMe.ILoveMyFonts!
BothTMobileAssociates
ShouldBeCommendedForTheir
CustomerServiceAssistance.
IAmSoHappyWithMyNewPhone
&HadAGreat&PleasantExperience
ThankYou!
MaryJoNaegele

We went to the TMobile in Centerra, Store 228 and spoke to Alex.  We weren't sure if we wanted to purchase a new phone because one of ours was always in need of repair.  He showed us every option from trading in to buying a new one, to adding a line.  He was so patient, respectful and professional.  We have to let T-Mobile know that Alex should be commended.  We did buy a new phone and he did explain everything to us.  We have been with T-Mobile for 24 years from when it was Voicestream.  Thank you Alex

I have been using T-Mobile for years and years and when I canceled they assured me I would get my refund within 30 days .It’s been nine months I typically don’t like putting people down but their customer service representatives lie to your face. They are thieves , Despicable liars and their  service is inferior to Verizon . Do not get lured in by false ads , it’s a big scam company who should be out of business

Just an FYI if you change from T-Mobile even a day after their statement cycles you will be charged for the full month cycle. “It’s in their policy.” To take $200 (or whatever your bill is) from you if you switch services.

I been fighting with t-mobil for the last two weeks over a phone that I ordered but never received, when I called back they have no record of ordering a phone,  this company sucks really bad , I hate t-mobil!!!!!!!!! Spoke to a customer agent Ralz from some foreign country and he refused to give me his employee ID number so when I asked for a supervisor he refused that option aswell, im changing carriers because of service like that...never had that problem with sprint.

Negative stars if I could. I was with Sprint before and had less problems, now that they took over, cell service at my home dropped like a Rick. No service, can't call out, no text consistently at my own home in a big city. Take it I don't live far far away with no cell towers. They keep this up and there business will be shuttered not now but down the road if all customers experience these things constantly. I am seriously thinking about switching after being with Sprint for 15 years. It almost sounds like they want us existing customers to leave, well keep this up and millions will. BAD CELL SERVICE AND TERRIBLE CUSTOMER SERVICE BEING TOSSED AROUND.

Getting new service was so non hassle. So much that I ordered a phone for my daughter and then after receiving hers I ordered myself one. I was a Sprint customer for 10+ years and it was overall good service. I appreciate a company that appreciates my business like what I’ve seen so far with with you.

I was a Sprint customer for years with no problem. Since Tmobile took over, my service is horrible. When you call their tech support you get someone from a foreign country you can't understand. They tell you their working on the problem and will call back. That never happens. Your complaint goes in the garbage can. I'm going to Verizon. Had enough!

Since the Tmobile take over of Sprint, service is so bad. I've been with Sprint for 22 years and have never had this problem with dropped calls, texts that will not go through and the only way to use the web is to use my Comcast. If you have a choice, and we do, go somewhere else. It all went down hill when I got my sim card, and alllllllll the Sprint apps were gone and it opened up with TMobile ....ugh

T-Mobile is garbage! I have tried to get service at my home in Bellevue WA. Minutes away from their USA headquarters and nothing! I usually have zero bars at my home and only get service by piggybacking on my Comcast internet. Right now I’m at the Westin in Boston and have 1 bar. I can text but no one can hear me on phone calls. Incredible T-Mobile has such crappy coverage!!!! I can’t believe I have them for my service, embarrassing!!!!!

Horrible, there is no other way to describe them. They have been hacked 5 times in the past 4 years, the latest hack is because they failed to secure (with a password) a router and cyber criminals accessed over 50 million customers (current and past) personal data (Name, address, SS #, Drivers License Number, Date of Birth, etc... 100% of each customers personal data...EVERYTHING).  My phone is blowing up with spam calls and I'm waiting for criminals to take out loans in my name, use my identify to commit other crimes.   T-Mobile SUCKS.

Husband and I bought 2 phones, transferring from Verizon. I went on my own to buy 2 more for my children but since it was his name on the account and he wasn't with me, I wasn't allowed.  I felt like the little woman who wasn't allowed to spend money without her husband's permission.  It didn't matter that it is my account, too - or that I know my pin number - or that I know my husband's SS# or any other pertinent information.  They needed to talk to him and make sure it was okay that I was a user on my own account.  Unbelievably crappy service. It didn't matter to the customer service rep that he works in a job that he can't just pop on the phone and give permission.  It didn't matter that it was my credit card that paid for the first 2 phones.  Nothing mattered but getting his permission.

I never received my SIM card in the mail. I contacted them multiple times and each time they essentially said they had no idea where my sim card was and never offered any solutions.

Follow-up: 10 days after, I spent two and half hours on hold and when I finally reached someone, they hung-up on me.
 Their customer service is terrible.

This is possibly the worst phone company I have ever had the chance of using.the only company where you can have full bars but can’t access any of the apps or internet. Truly extremely disappointed for it being considered the “contender” against Verizon for sure switching back from this garbage.

Unfortunately switched over to TMobile and since then have been overcharged, experiencing continual dropped calls and no service, unbelievably slow data (watching the screen load) and the screen does not function properly when I press it (Apple 12 Pro Max) and have now spent over 2 hours over several calls attempting to rectify these issues.

Do not switch to TMobile. I switched from Boost because boost sucks too but they were better than TMobile. I never have service. My calls drop and I pay $96 for magenta Max plan. I am so mad at myself for going to them without researching first! I'll be switching to AT&T!
Zero service.

I really loved Sprint services, I hate the merge with T-Mobile. The services is horrible, places I need my phone doesn't work 5g 👎🏽👎🏽. You all talk about the service being great but far from it. IT NEEDS TO IMPROVE.....SO DISAPPOINTED. I MISS SPRINT

I would rate zero but not an option. This team of experts can't answer a call. There service is shoddy and impossible to get help or answers. Constant dropped calls. There estimated wait time is 20times the estimated wait time. Ridiculous for a 2021 company in technology.
Kept waited for hours with no result.
I wonder if anyone ever get their call answered. Always wait, 45 min, 90min. I scheduled a call back, they did, and they hand it up so fast like in 1 second. Amazing.

I want to give accolades to 3 people at the Ames store ABSN store #402.573.5678 in Omaha, NE. Amir, Taleigha & Robin did so much to resolve my issues. This was my 2nd time in the store for this issue. Amir was helping me and through no fault of his own, I was overwhelmed with the whole process of trying to get a new phone. WiFi was terribly slow so he couldn't access my account. My phone was too old for a TMobile sim card. A new phone would cause problems for the other two people on the account. It just wasn't good so, I left. Today was stellar! Amir remembered me so I didn't have repeat my issue. WiFi was working so he quickly found my account & introduced the phone I was eligible for. He and Taleigha and Robin worked together to quickly get my new phone working. I am a senior citizen & they took the time explain things to me. They didn't make me feel stupid. They even brought me a chair to sit in while things were getting done. I expressed that with the new phone, I might be able to hear my phone music through my car. They offered to set it up. They'd already done enough so I talked them out of it. I walked out of the store floating on cloud nine. Thanks to these 3 people.

Run away from Tmobile. They liiiiiiieeeeee. Opening my acc and gets a deal and trade in for both 2 phones. They mess up the order and playing me all the way, received a different answer and the deal wasn’t applied as promised on their deal site. T Mobile is a garbage liiiiiaarrrr. Don’t trust customer service they make up  the answer and you need to call back again next time when things didn’t go right. Don’t trust the deal you lose money without you realizing. First time opening my account with TMobile and is not what I expected. I am upset, disappointed for the last 8th months was a disaster almost every month you need to call them for things and they will answer you like what you expected but the next time you call there a different bu***it they will tell you until you pissoff, I am canceling it and pay off my phones and move to different carrier. Never ever sign up with t mobile deals!!!!!!! Nor the service!!!!!!!! if I don’t have Wi-Fi TM will never gets connected to gets signals.

Would give a zero if I could Less then 6 weeks with my phone and first time with T-Mobile Phone stopped working Store where I bought it was no help They were very loud, ignorant an rude Customer service is even worse Gave me the run around Manufacturer of the phone were just as bad Long wait times Spent 270 for a price of trash Feel like I bought a lemon from a used car salesmen Will never deal with them again They SUCK!!!!!

Ever since Sprint has merged with T-Mobile my service has been awful. I eventually found out that T-Mobile put all of the sprint customers onto the bottom of the ladder and we have no prioritization. My internet and coverages has suffered through T-Mobile. Now they want to push me off of the sprint kick stater plan which is 35 bucks a month for unlimited talk text and web and are telling me to pay at least 85 for the magenta. I am going to another carrier and never coming back.

I recently found out from T-Mobile that there was a DATA BREACH and all of their customers private information was stolen and their reaction to this situation is ONLY 2 years of credit history protection where as the people who stole our social security and drivers license numbers will have this information forever to do what they want with and we will have to suffer because T-Mobile didn’t take the initiative to protect us and we are supposed to be their VALUED CUSTOMERS!! They provided a scam shield app that was supposed to protect us from scam calls but DIDN’T DO A THING to protect us against SCAM TEXT MESSAGES, I never did any except try to block them but these scam messages kept coming, I complained over and over but T-Mobile didn’t do anything and now our information is in the hands of people from other countries because they didn’t care enough to protect us completely through phone and text messages. T-Mobile says their outsourcing companies are called GLOBAL PARTNERS but I believe they are the GLOBAL ELITES who are trying to hurt and bring down this country and for those of you who do not believe this just wait until you get stuck with a bad credit rating and have to be responsible for all of the bills incurred in your name with your social security and drivers license numbers!!! Wake up people these cell phones companies are not working for us they are working for the very people who hate us!!

Worst customer service I’ve EVER experienced. Cancelled the internet service before I ever connected because the modem wouldn’t get a signal after following their set up directions. I got a bill for $55. Called, after 1 hour 14 min on hold they said they straightened it out. Today I got another bill for $15.01. I’ve been on hold WELL over 2 hours. Called from friends cell and pushed button for new service, got right through…clearly no one wants the new service and everyone is calling to complain or cancel. Of course that person couldn’t help me and guy even gave me a FAKE number telling me it was a “direct” number instead of the typical customer service number! It’s ridiculous. 
WORST COMPANY EVER!!!

T mobile sucks! The only reason I’m giving them one star is because I can’t give 0. I was a sprint customer and service was perfect where I live then T-Mobile took over and good service was replaced by dropped calls spotty or no coverage. Their 5G is twice as slow as my Sprint 4g was! I took my phone in multiple times. The tech changed my sim to a T-Mobile sim. Worse. Then the salesman talked my wife and I into paying off our phones and taking a “free” phone deal which really isn’t free because it obligates you to use their crap service for 24 months or you have to pay full price for your “free” phone if you want to discontinue. My advice. Choose anyone else. Shady business practices and bad product.

I went into the TMobile store to get specific help with my SD card.  My phone was no longer recognizing the card and after numerous attempts at reseating it which only ended in the phone asking me to reformat the card, which would wipe it clean, I decided to go to the store for help.  Well, the rep just reformatted the card and said she didn't.  Even after I showed her that all my data was gone from the SD card she still said she did nothing wrong and only synched the card to the phone.  I have been a TMobile customer for 19 years and I am totally disgusted at this point.  I have lost business docs, pictures, videos and the rep could care less.  She refused to call anyone or ask someone else for help to see if there was anything that could be done to salvage the card data.  Seriously amazed at how little these people know about their jobs.  Rethinking who my carrier should be.

These mental midgets  are as stupid as sprint. Their “ experts” are low paid bottom feeders that can’t figure out anything. Your on line waiting endlessly to get connected. Then your transferred to another “ expert” because the first one can’t do their job. Wait forever to get your call answered then deal with stupidity. 
All the advertising about great service is a LIE!
Expect a moron and you won’t be disappointed. I just switched from sprint and I hate them almost as much .

Ive worked with all carriers of cell phones through Samsung for 8 years. To migrate my sprint phone number to another tmobile account has been the worst possible process  I've ever seen. They made me pay half of the bill for the next month. Even though we just paid a week ago. They keep giving me a new hoop to jump through when I've been trying to do this for two weeks. No one knows what theyre doing here.

Worst service ever stay away dead zones everywhere calls don’t come in text don’t come in can’t wait to leave after paying off my phone go to Comcast xfinity mobile it’s much better and lower prices I literally pay to have no service with T-Mobile

This company is more corrupt. I was told that if I gave them a try and the service was not good that they would not charge me. That was a lie. Not only did they charge me but the also did not cancel my account when I requested just to run up my bill. I reached out to them numerous times to straighten this out. They keep telling me that they would look into it and call me back. Of course I never received a call back not one time out of the five times I called about the same issue.

I am new to T-mobile.  I am coming from verizon, the most awful copany in cell service I have ever dealt with.  I called them and could not get a person.  They force the chat option where robots answer questions and if you can get a human, they spew the same garbage.  I paid $70 and stayed one month.  I called T-mobile, got a person right away. Normal for new service.  I had to call customer service through 611 and got a person pretty quickly.  The first gal did not get my issue taken care of but since I found 2 more things I needed help with, I got someone who went beyound and called verizon to get my account number to port my number properly.  The call hung up immediately when the port was successful.  Took a little time but I got it done.  In fact, just a second ago I got a call verifying everything I needed taken care was taken care of.  Number  ported, got my netflix, and hotspot now works.  I got a hold of a person.  Verizon no longer answers their phones at the stores.  This even says so on thier answering machine on all 3 stores in Lincoln NE.  I went in and was told to open in a browser and figure it out.  I walked out.  Very disappointing experience.  I spent hours trying to sinc the ap and the phone and while their end said it was successful, I could not log in no matter how many magic numbers they sent me.  Nothing worked.  The phone will NOT work without the ap.  I had to enter in all information 17 times the first time and it kept erasing everything and putting me back to the beginning.  I only did it 6 times the second before I called T-mobile.  The ap is optional with T-mobile.  Maybe it is not all that good or maybe it is.  Either way I can chat from my laptop if I have the time or I can dial 611 and spend a couple minutes getting around their answering system to see if it is a quick fix or your issue is on their phone menu. The first rep tried and was really nice. She might be new.  The second rep spoke better English and that helps because I am hard of hearing.  The 3rd rep was in chat and she helped me with the rest of set up.  Then she called back to make sure I got everything I needed for my new service.  So far so good.  I rarely call a cell phone carrier so I hope to have many years of good service.  Way better than verizon in customer service.  I can't tell you anything about cell service because I was not able to use any other service other than call or text and a lot of the time they wanted me to connect to the internet and data did not work.  Very happy right now.

Called IT tech support and was locked into sales pitches for over an hour. My problem was not resolved and the customer service representatives were exceedingly unhelpful. I was transferred multiple times and told to just restart my device about 5 times during the whole ordeal. 
-Bad customer service 
-Only focused on new sales not existing plan holders 
-Terrible connection overall to boot

I recently signed up for the T-Mobile Home Internet and it was the worse experience ever.
Called on Aug 10th, I gave them a call to try and get an estimated speed I would be getting at my address. The rep just read off the ad. Pretty much didn't provide any info I didn't already know.
Eventually, I told her I would like to go ahead and proceed, she tells me the modem is on backorder so it can be between Aug 15-Sep 13th when I could be getting the modem and that it would be $50. I told her I was cool with that and she gave me the order# and went on about my business.
On Aug 26th, the modem comes in. I unboxed it, plugged it in, and follow the instructions. Wifi connected, but not to internet. after fiddling it for a few moments longer I decided to call the tech. I told the tech that I already have everything set up, connected to wifi, but no internet. and he starts with with "first take the modem out of the box" .........dude I already did that. then he tells me to move the modem around to find the best signal.......... already did that. 28 minutes later, he tells me the reason I'm not connected to the internet was because the engineers are currently working on the network. also with no time frame what that would be completed. I was like whatever, odd that my phone, which is also with T-mobile could connect to the network and get internet connection.
The next day on Aug 27, I was obvious unsatisfied with the responds I got the previous day so during my lunch break, I give the tech a call back. 1st guy I spoke to, I had high hopes for because he asked for the modem's IMEI#. He then tells me my modem is not activated yet and would need to have a phone line and had to get transfer over to another dept to do so.
I then get a lady and I tell her that the previous guy told me I need to get a line activated for my home internet. after a few minutes she asked me where I got the modem. I looked at the shipping label and told her what I saw on there. She then said that I need to get a modem from them and that she will send me a new one free of charge. I thought whatever and okay. but then she she told me about the backorder and it would take this long. I was like hold up. I do have an order number. gave that to her and then she proceed to tell me that the person that added the order didn't add the line to the account.
after awhile, she then says I would need to get transfer over to the home internet tech to get it activated and that she will explain everything to the next tech before transferring me over. which I thought was cool since my first transfer that didn't happen and I had to re-explain everything.
the 3rd rep for the day did all he could do and said that it took 24 hours before he can finish activating. He then verifies my number and confirmed that I would be near my phone to answer his call the next day. All and all I just wasted my lunch hour on the phone with them and still no T-mobile Home Internet service.
Aug 28th, I waited my whole lunch hour and no phone call. so I had to wait a couple hours after to call during my last break. I called and told the guy,  hey I have everything set up. the guy from yesterday says you just need to finalize the activation. after about a 15 minute phone call, he tells me his supervisor will have to call me later to proceed with the activation. I tell him that the supervisor will have to call me after 5pm CST because that is when I get off work.
after around 5:40, but I checked my T-mobile account and saw some changes were done and that the modem received a text message with the new phone number.
I speed tested the connection and got less than 2 mbps. I took my phone off wifi and got low 20s. I connected to Uverse wifi and got low 30s.
I decided to restart the modem and tried again. everything under 10s, so I decided I should just try back later since a lot of reviews says it could slow down during peak hours.
So around 10pm til 11pm I speed tested and got low 20s. which I was not impress since it doesn't even get what I am already currently getting with Uverse. called it a night and went to bed
The next Morning on Aug 29th, I tested it and got over 100 mbps. Which I was excited about. But when I logged on to my account later in the day and saw what I was being charged .... $60 after  $5 off with auto pay.
I called to complain, but they just tell me its supposed to be $60 and didn't really care that someone told me wrong. With all the frustration the last few days, I just told the to cancel the service. I'll just wait til Uverse get their fiber up and pay for reliability of fiber and not have to worry about the fluctuation that would be expected with the T-Mobile home internet services

I went to T-Mobile, because I found out Verizon Wireless supported the Black Lives Matter Movement, which is a Marxist terrorist organization.  After getting T-Mobile, when I talked on the phone there were a lot of moments where I could not hear the person talking on the other side of the call.  

Towards the end of having T-Mobile, this happened to 80% of my phone calls.  I called 611 and they tried to repair the situation, but it was to no avail.  They even suggested the problem was caused by maintenance on a tower.  But when I found out that the tower had been fixed and I still couldn’t hear the phone calls I was getting, I went back to Verizon Wireless.

When I went to get a refund for funds in my account I paid in advanced, they sent me a “debit card” costing $3.00 a month that my bank did not honor.  I was livid and demanded that they send me a check.  After contacting them four times going into a store, they finally agreed to give me a check, which is what normal companies do when you terminate service with them and there is a credit to the account.

I strongly discourage anyone using T-Mobile, and if you choose T-Mobile don’t have extra money in the account if you want to see it again.
